== Accessing the contents menu ==

The contents menu in the [[Contents|'''Contents''' window]] can be used for operations on the [[Glossary:Knowledge_tree|knowledge tree]]. To open this menu:

* press ''Shift+F10'' in the [[Contents|'''Contents''' window]],
* right-click over the selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] of the [[Glossary:Knowledge_tree|knowledge tree]], or
* press ''Apps Key'' ([[Image:Application key.gif|Application key allows you to open a pop-up menu relevant for the current context]]) (context menu key left of the right ''Ctrl'' key).

== Contents menu items ==

The contents menu includes the following options: 

=== New ===

*'''New''' - add new [[Glossary:Element|elements]]
**'''Item''' - create a new [[Glossary:Item|item]] as a [[Glossary:Child|child]] of the selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]
**'''Topic''' - create a new [[Glossary:Topic|topic]] as a [[Glossary:Child|child]] of the selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]
**'''Add child''' - add a new [[Glossary:Element|element]] as a child of the currently selected element. A child is an element that is one level down in the [[Glossary:Knowledge_tree|knowledge tree]]. The type of the [[Glossary:Element|element]] is determined by the default type in the '''Element type''' combo box (at the bottom of the [[Contents|'''Contents''' window]]) 
**'''Insert sibling''' (''Ins'') - insert a new [[Glossary:Element|element]] in the place of the currently selected element (the current element moves one position down). The type of the new [[Glossary:Element|element]] is determined by the default type in the '''Element type''' combo box (at the bottom of the [[Contents|'''Contents''' window]]) 
**'''Duplicate''' (''Alt+D'') - duplicate the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]] and attach the duplicate as a [[Glossary:Child|child]] of the duplicated element 
**'''Folder''' - create a [[Glossary:Dismissed_element|dismissed]] [[Glossary:Template|template]]-less [[Glossary:Topic|topic]], which can act as a folder for holding other [[Glossary:Element|elements]]

=== View ===

*'''View''' 
**'''Element''' (''Shift+Enter'') - bring the [[element window]] to the front with the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]
**'''<span id="Branch">Branch</span>''' (''Ctrl+Space'') - put all [[Glossary:Element|elements]] belonging to the selected [[Glossary:Branch|branches]] in the [[Browser|element browser]]
**'''Descendants''' - put all [[Glossary#Descendant|descendants]] of the selected [[Glossary:Branch|branches]] in the [[Browser|element browser]] (see: [https://supermemopedia.com/wiki/Difference_between_View_Branch_and_View_Descendants_in_Contents_Menu interpretation])
**'''Selection '''- view all selected [[Glossary:Element|elements]] in the [[Browser|element browser]]
**'''Children''' - put all [[Glossary:Child|children]] of the currently selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] in the [[Browser|element browser]] 
**'''Map''' - view the currently [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] as a map
**'''Parent''' (''Ctrl+Up'') - go to the parent element of the presently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]
**'''First child''' - go to the first [[Glossary:Child|child]] of the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]
**'''Last child''' (''Ctrl+Down'') - go to the last [[Glossary:Child|child]] of the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]
**'''Collapse''' (''Ctrl+Alt+Enter'') - collapse the current [[Glossary:Branch|branch]]

=== Rename ===

*'''Rename''' - change the title of the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]

=== Copy ===

*'''Copy''' - copy the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]] to the clipboard

=== Paste ===

*'''Paste''' - paste the [[Glossary:Element|element]] from the clipboard (previously put there with '''Copy''' above)

=== Delete ===

*'''Delete''' - delete the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]] and all its [[Glossary#Child|children]] and [[Glossary#Descendant|descendants]] (if any)

=== Move ===

*'''Move''' (''Shift+Ctrl+V'') - move the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]] to a new location in the [[Glossary:Knowledge_tree|tree]]. Use '''Accept''' (at the bottom of the window) or double-click to point to the new location for the [[Glossary:Element|element]]

=== Learn ===

*[[Subset learning|'''Learn''']] (''Ctrl+L'') - make repetitions on the currently selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] and memorize [[Glossary:Pending_element|pending elements]] belonging to the branch

=== Dismiss ===

*'''Dismiss''' (''Ctrl+D'') - [[Glossary:Dismiss|dismiss]] the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]

=== Search ===

*'''Search''' 
**'''Find''' - find a string in the first title in expanded [[Glossary:Branch|branches]] below the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]]
**'''Find next''' (''F3'') - repeat the last search done with '''Search : Find''' above
**'''Search branch''' (''Ctrl+F'') - search for a text in the selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]]. All texts are considered in search (i.e. not only titles)

=== Concept ===

*'''Concept''' - options for managing [[Glossary:Concept|concepts]] 
**'''<span id="Create_new_category">Create</span>''' (''Ctrl+K'') - create a new [[Glossary:Concept|concept]] with the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]] as the [[Glossary:Root_node|root]]. The [[Glossary:Concept|concept]] will take the title of its [[Glossary:Root_node|root element]] as its name. You can change that to a shorter or more mnemonic name. The currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]] will also become the first [[Glossary:Hook_node|hook]] of the [[Glossary:Concept|concept]]
**'''<span id="Make_root">Set root</span>''' - make the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]] the [[Glossary:Root_node|root]] of the current [[Glossary:Concept_group|concept group]] (the current [[Glossary:Concept_group|concept group]] is displayed on the [[navigation bar]] in the [[element window]]). The [[Glossary:Root_node|root]] is the [[Glossary:Element|element]] that is the [[Glossary:Ancestor|ascendant]] of all elements in the [[Glossary:Concept_group|concept group]]
**'''<span id="Make_hook">Set hook</span>''' - make the currently selected [[Glossary:Element|element]] the [[Glossary:Hook_node|hook]] of the current [[Glossary:Concept_group|concept group]] (the current concept group is displayed on the [[navigation bar]] in the [[element window]]). Once you change the [[Glossary:Hook_node|hook]], all newly added [[Glossary:Element|elements]] will be [[Glossary:Child|children]] of the hook (only within the current [[Glossary:Concept_group|concept group]])

=== Export ===

*'''Export''' 
**'''Transfer elements''' - transfer the entire [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] to another [[Glossary:Collection|collection]]. This option is similar to '''[[File menu|File]] : [[File menu#Merge collection|Merge collection]]'''; however, only the selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] is transferred (as opposed to the entire [[Glossary:Collection|collection]])
**'''Document''' - export all texts from the currently selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] as a single HTML document 
**'''Q&amp;A text file''' - export all [[Glossary:Item|items]] from the currently selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] to a Q&amp;A text file 
**'''XML''' - export all [[Glossary:Element|elements]] from the currently selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] in the XML format that can be imported to other SuperMemos. XML files may also be imported back to SuperMemo for Windows 
**'''HTML''' - export the currently selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] to HTML. Not all [[Glossary:Component|component]] types can be exported. The component layout is not preserved 
**'''Source text''' - export the currently selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] source text in the text format 
**'''Subset''' - export a [[Glossary:Subset|subset]] as text. This option is the same as '''Export : Source text''' with this exception that only the [[Glossary:Element|elements]] belonging to a selected [[Glossary:Subset|subset]] are exported. Read more: [[Using subsets]] 
**'''Learning data''' - export the learning process from all [[Glossary:Element|elements]] in the currently selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]]. You can later use '''[[File menu|File]] : [[File menu#Import|Import]] : Learning process''' to import such a file to the same [[Glossary:Collection|collection]] or a subset collection derived from the original collection (e.g. generated to make repetitions on a notebook with limited space or performance)

=== Tools ===

*'''Tools''' 
** '''Name node''' - automatically assign a name and date to an import folder using the titles of imported children
**'''Regroup in folders''' - group all [[Glossary:Child|children]] in the [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] into a number of branches with equal number of children. Do not affect further [[Glossary:Descendant|descendants]]. Unlike '''Arrange in folders''' below, this option preserves the structure of knowledge generated with [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]] 
**'''<span id="Arrange">Arrange in folders</span>''' - group all [[Glossary:Element|elements]] in the [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] into a number of branches with equal number of [[Glossary:Child|children]]. For example, if your [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] contains 100 [[Glossary:Element|elements]], you can use '''Arrange in folders''' to create 10 empty elements used as placeholders for the remaining elements. Each of those newly created [[Glossary:Element|elements]] will host 10 [[Glossary:Child|children]]. Thus all your 100 [[Glossary:Element|elements]] will be grouped in 10 [[Glossary:Branch|branches]] with 10 [[Glossary:Child|children]] each
**'''Flatten''' - convert a branched [[Glossary:Node|tree node]] into a flat linear structure of [[Glossary:Sibling|sibling]]s 
**'''Random test''' (''F11'') - run a random test on all [[Glossary:Element|elements]] in the selected [[Glossary:Branch|branch]]

=== Font ===

*'''Font''' - change the font used in the [[Contents|'''Contents''' window]]

=== Process branch> ===

*'''Process branch>''' - process all [[Glossary:Element|elements]] in a given [[Glossary:Branch|branch]] using options available from the [[Subset operations|subset processing menu]]

=== Close ===

*'''Close''' (''Ctrl+F4'') - close the [[Contents|'''Contents''' window]]. You can re-open it by choosing '''Contents''' in the [[element window]]
